description: Evals for refactoring the YOLO FastAPI data layer from raw sqlite3 to SQLAlchemy ORM.

cases.prompt: Refactor services/yolo/app.py so the data layer uses SQLAlchemy ORM instead of raw sqlite3, without changing any existing API behavior, status codes, or response body structures.
cases.expected_output: A new services/yolo/db.py defines Base, engine, SessionLocal, get_db, and the PredictionSession and DetectionObject models. app.py no longer imports sqlite3; all endpoints that access the database use db: Session = Depends(get_db). Every existing endpoint returns the same JSON shape and status codes as before.
assertions: services/yolo/db.py exists and defines Base, engine, SessionLocal, and get_db
No occurrence of 'import sqlite3' remains in services/yolo/
No raw SQL strings (CREATE TABLE, INSERT INTO, SELECT, DELETE) or conn.execute calls remain
Endpoints accessing the DB declare 'db: Session = Depends(get_db)'
GET /prediction/{uid} still returns 404 with detail 'Prediction not found' when missing
Response body keys (uid, timestamp, original_image, predicted_image, detection_objects) are unchanged

cases.prompt: Add a new endpoint GET /predictions/count that returns the total number of prediction sessions, using SQLAlchemy and FastAPI dependency injection.
cases.expected_output: A new endpoint uses db: Session = Depends(get_db) and an ORM query (e.g. db.query(PredictionSession).count()) to return JSON like {"count": <int>}. No raw SQL is used, and existing endpoints are untouched.
assertions: A GET /predictions/count endpoint is added
The endpoint signature includes 'db: Session = Depends(get_db)'
The count is computed via an ORM query, not a raw SQL string
The endpoint returns a JSON object containing a numeric 'count' field with status 200
Existing endpoints and their response shapes remain unchanged

cases.prompt: Add a new SQLAlchemy model and table named prediction_tags with columns id (integer primary key) and prediction_uid (foreign key to prediction_sessions.uid) and tag (text), wired into Base so it is created by init_db().
cases.expected_output: A new ORM model maps to a prediction_tags table with the requested columns and foreign key. The table is registered on Base.metadata and created via Base.metadata.create_all in init_db(). No raw CREATE TABLE statement is used.
assertions: A new model class maps to __tablename__ = 'prediction_tags'
The model defines id, prediction_uid (ForeignKey to prediction_sessions.uid), and tag columns
The table is created through Base.metadata.create_all, not a raw CREATE TABLE string
No raw SQL is introduced for the new table

cases.prompt: Add an endpoint DELETE /prediction/{uid} that deletes a prediction session and its detection objects using SQLAlchemy, returning 404 if the session does not exist.
cases.expected_output: The endpoint uses db: Session = Depends(get_db), loads the PredictionSession via an ORM query, deletes the related DetectionObject rows and the session through the ORM (db.delete / query.delete), commits, and raises HTTPException(status_code=404) when the uid is not found. No raw DELETE SQL is used.
assertions: A DELETE /prediction/{uid} endpoint is added
The endpoint uses 'db: Session = Depends(get_db)'
Deletion is performed via ORM (db.delete or query.delete), not a raw DELETE statement
Detection objects related to the uid are also removed
Returns 404 when the prediction session does not exist
Returns a 2xx status on successful deletion

cases.prompt: Make the YOLO data layer support both SQLite for local development and PostgreSQL in production, configured entirely through environment variables.
cases.expected_output: The engine URL is read from a DATABASE_URL environment variable defaulting to a SQLite URL (sqlite:///./predictions.db). SQLite-only connect args (check_same_thread) are applied only for SQLite URLs. requirements.txt includes sqlalchemy and a PostgreSQL driver (psycopg2-binary). Switching DATABASE_URL to a postgresql:// URL requires no code changes.
assertions: DATABASE_URL is read from os.environ with a SQLite default
check_same_thread connect arg is applied only for SQLite URLs
services/yolo/requirements.txt includes sqlalchemy and a PostgreSQL driver (e.g. psycopg2-binary)
No database credentials or engine URLs are hardcoded for PostgreSQL
Switching to a postgresql:// DATABASE_URL requires no source code changes

cases.prompt: Update services/yolo/tests so they use a temporary SQLite database and never touch the real database, and so any API test that triggers inference mocks the YOLO model and asserts both status codes and response body structures.
cases.expected_output: Tests create a temporary SQLite database (e.g. under tmp_path) and override the get_db dependency so the real predictions.db is never used. Tests that hit /predict mock the YOLO model instead of loading real weights, and assert both the HTTP status code and the JSON response body keys/structure.
assertions: Tests build a temporary SQLite database (e.g. via tmp_path) and override get_db
The real predictions.db / production database is never used in tests
The YOLO model is mocked for inference-triggering tests (no real weights loaded)
API tests assert both the status code and the response body structure
pytest passes with the refactored SQLAlchemy data layer
